utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hi billy how are you what's been going on the medical assistant told me that you're having some difficulty urinating

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah yeah i i did n't really wan na come in to talk about it's kinda weird but i think probably over the last six months i'm just not peeing right it just does n't seem to be normal

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so let's talk a little bit about that now is your is your stream is your urination stream weak

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i'd probably say so

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and do you feel like you're emptying your bladder fully or do you feel like you still have some urine left in there when you when you finish

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: most of the times i'm okay but sometimes if i stand there long enough i i can kinda go a little bit more so it's taking a while actually to just go to the bathroom

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and are you waking up at night to go to the bathroom does it impact your sleep

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah i try to empty my bladder now right before i go to bed and and not drink anything but i'm still probably getting up three or four times a night to go to the bed

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so you're getting up about three or four times a night and and how long has this been going on you said for about six months

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah six months to like this and it's probably been a little bit worse over the last six months and maybe it's been longer i just did n't want to bring it up

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay so you think it's been going on longer okay alright now how about have you had any burning when you urinate at all

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no it i do n't think it burns

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: no burning when you urinate okay and and any other any other issues any problems with your bowels any constipation issues

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hmmm no i i i had diarrhea last week but i think i ate something bad

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and ever have you ever had any issues where you had what we call urinary retention where you could n't pee and you needed to have like a catheter inserted

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: my gosh no

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'll do that

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright and have you ever seen a urologist i do n't think so you've been my patient for a while i do n't remember ever sending you but have you ever seen one

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i do n't think so

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay now tell me how are you doing with your with your heart when was the last time you saw doctor moore the cardiologist i know that you had the the stent placed in your right coronary artery about what was that twenty eighteen

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah sounds about right i think i just saw him in november he said everything was okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: he said everything was okay alright and so you have n't had any chest pain or shortness of breath you're still walking around doing your activities of daily living are you exercising

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: kind of

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: kind of okay now from what i remember i remember you being a big college football fan are you as excited as i am that georgia beat alabama in the national championships

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah yeah i'm super excited

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you do n't really seem that excited

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: get the problem fixed because i have to be able to sit there and watch the whole game

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah i i really do n't like nick saving i'm so i'm super happy that that the dogs pulled it out

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i do n't know if we can do friends anymore

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: are you in alabama fan

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: maybe i'm actually originally not from georgia so

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright well i mean i i'm i'm a long horns fan but anyway well i digress let's talk a little bit about your diabetes how are how are you doing with your sugars are you watching your diet

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm trying to yeah i think they are okay

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and are you still taking the metformin

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yep

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: you are okay alright now i wan na go ahead and just move on to a quick physical exam okay i'm gon na be calling out some of my exam findings and i'm gon na let you know what that means when i'm done okay alright i do have to do a rectal exam i apologize i'm just gon na be calling it out what what i what i appreciate okay so on your heart exam i do appreciate a slight three out of six systolic ejection murmur hurt at the left base on your lung exam your lungs are clear to auscultation bilaterally on your abdominal exam your abdomen is nontender and nondistended i do n't appreciate any masses or any rebound or guarding on your prostate exam i do appreciate an enlarged prostate i do n't appreciate any masses on physical exam so what what does that mean billy so that ultimately means that you know everything looks good you know you have that little heart murmur which i believe you you've had in the past but we're gon na go ahead and look into that you know your prostate seems a little bit enlarged to me on physical exam so let's talk about how we can go about and and remedy that okay so for your first problem of this you know difficulty urinating i wan na go ahead and just order some routine labs i wan na get a a psa that kind of that ultimately kinda looks for prostate cancer issues which i do n't think you have because we did n't really appreciate that on physical exam i wan na go ahead and we can try to start you on what we call flomax zero point four milligrams once a day you should take it at night because it can cause people to get a little bit dizzy if they take it in the morning so i would take it at night and i wan na go ahead and refer you to a urologist just to look into this more so we can go ahead and and get this problem solved for you okay i'm also gon na go ahead and just order some routine blood tests just to make sure that we are not missing anything do you have any questions about that and i wan na go ahead and order a urinalysis and a urine culture

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah so sounds good have you seen that commercial for that super batter prostate stuff does that work

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: well i think the data it's it's i'm not really sure if it works or not i'm not that familiar with it let's just go ahead and stick with flomax and that's why we are gon na refer you to the urologist so that they can go ahead and talk to you about you know the most current treatment options for you okay

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: alright

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: alright for your second problem of your coronary artery disease i wan na go ahead and order an echocardiogram just to follow up on that heart murmur that you had and i wan na go ahead and continue you on the lipitor forty milligrams a day and the aspirin and the metoprolol and i wan na go ahead and order a lipid panel any questions about that

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: nope

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay and then for your third problem of your diabetes it sounds like you're doing really well let's go ahead and continue you on the metformin a thousand milligrams twice a day we will go ahead and order a hemoglobin a1c to see if we need to make any adjustments to that and i'm gon na see you again in about three to four weeks okay i want you to call me or message me in the patient portal if you have any concerns

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: alright when is the urologist gon na call me

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm gon na reach out i'm gon na reach out to them now and see if they can get you in this week

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sounds good

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay alright well great it was good to see you bye

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i could just hit it and i can just talk and then i'm just

detail_list.summary: Difficulty urinating.
The patient reports a six-month history of difficulty urinating.
The doctor asks if the patient's urinary stream is weak.
The patient confirms his urinary stream is weak.
The doctor asks if the patient feels he is emptying his bladder fully.
The patient reports it takes a long time to urinate and he sometimes feels he needs to stand longer to finish.
The doctor asks if the patient wakes up at night to urinate.
The patient reports waking up three to four times a night to urinate.
The patient confirms the urinary symptoms have persisted for six months.
The patient notes the urinary symptoms have worsened over the last six months.
The doctor asks if the patient has any dysuria.
The patient denies dysuria.
The doctor asks if the patient has any bowel issues or constipation.
The patient denies chronic bowel issues, noting only a recent episode of diarrhea.
The doctor asks if the patient has a history of urinary retention requiring catheterization.
The patient denies a history of urinary retention.
The doctor asks if the patient has ever seen a urologist.
The patient denies having seen a urologist.
Coronary artery disease.
The patient has a history of coronary artery disease with a stent placed in 2018.
The patient reports a recent follow-up with his cardiologist in November.
The doctor asks if the patient has chest pain or shortness of breath and if he is exercising.
The patient reports that he tries to exercise.
Diabetes.
The doctor asks how the patient is managing his diabetes and diet.
The patient reports he is trying to manage his diet and believes his blood sugars are okay.
The doctor asks if the patient is still taking metformin.
The patient confirms he is taking metformin.
Physical exam findings include a 3/6 systolic ejection murmur, clear lungs, nontender abdomen, and an enlarged prostate.
The doctor will order a PSA, urinalysis, urine culture, and refer the patient to a urologist.
The doctor prescribes Flomax 0.4 mg once daily at night.
The patient asks about the efficacy of over-the-counter prostate supplements.
The doctor advises sticking to the prescribed Flomax and discussing treatment options with the urologist.
The doctor will order an echocardiogram and a lipid panel.
The patient will continue Lipitor 40 mg daily, aspirin, and metoprolol.
The patient will continue metformin 1000 mg twice daily.
The doctor will order a hemoglobin A1c and schedule a follow-up in three to four weeks.
The patient asks when the urologist will contact him.
The doctor will contact the urologist to attempt to schedule an appointment for this week.
